intro: Sarcolophium suberosum, also known as Kurchi or Puncture Vine, is a plant species used in traditional medicine, particularly in Ayurvedic and Unani practices. It is valued for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of various skin conditions, such as eczema and psoriasis, and its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. The bioactive constituents of Sarcolophium suberosum include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Sarcolophium suberosum is commonly used to prepare various herbal preparations, including decoctions, infusions, and powdered extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
